summary: zer0one-dnslog exfiltrates cloud metadata credentials to Burp Collaborator on install
details: The package's postinstall.js, executed automatically on `npm install`, curls cloud-provider instance metadata services — AWS IAM security-credentials at 169.254.169.254, Aliyun metadata at 100.100.100.200, Tencent metadata at metadata.tencentyun.com / 169.254.0.23, and an internal Meituan SSRF endpoint — saves the responses to /tmp, and then POSTs the concatenated body together with a `ls -la /data/` directory listing to http://h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com/metadata (a Burp Collaborator subdomain controlled by the operator). On any cloud build host or CI runner with an attached IAM role, this harvests live STS credentials and ships them to an attacker-controlled out-of-band channel. The package's stated purpose in package.json is `"A simple date formatting utility"` with keywords `date`, `format`, `util`, and its only library code is an `index.js` exporting a trivial `hello()` — a cover story bearing no relationship to the postinstall behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
